contents: Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc Basics.Equivalences.
Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc Basics.Equivalences.
Require Import Types.Unit Types.Paths Types.Universe Types.Sigma.

(** * Homotopy Cofibers *)

(** ** Definition *)

(** Homotopy cofibers or mapping cones are spaces constructed from a map [f : X -> Y] by contracting the image of [f] inside [Y] to a point. They can be thought of as a kind of coimage or quotient. *)

Definition Cofiber {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y)
  := Pushout f (const_tt X).

(** *** Constructors *)

(** Any element of [Y] can be included in the cofiber. *)

Definition cofib {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) : Y -> Cofiber f
  := pushl.
(** We have a distinguished point in the cofiber, where the image of [f] is contracted to. *)

Definition cf_apex {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) : Cofiber f
  := pushr tt.
(** Given an element [x : X], we have the path [cfglue f x] from the [f x] to the [cf_apex]. *)

Definition cfglue {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) (x : X) : cofib f (f x) = cf_apex f
  := pglue _.

(** *** Induction and recursion principles *)

(** The recursion principle for the cofiber of [f : X -> Y] requires a function [g : Y -> Z] such that [g o f] is null homotopic, i.e. constant. *)

Definition cofiber_rec {X Y Z : Type} (f : X -> Y) (g : Y -> Z)
  (null : NullHomotopy (g o f))
  : Cofiber f -> Z.

goals.conclusion: Cofiber f -> Z
Cofiber f -> Z
Y -> Z
Unit -> Z
forall a : X, ?pushb (f a) = ?pushc (const_tt X a)
Y -> Z
Unit -> Z
forall a : X, g (f a) = unit_name null.1 (const_tt X a)
g (f a) = unit_name null.1 (const_tt X a)

contents: (** The induction principle is similar, although requires a dependent form of null homotopy. *)

Definition cofiber_ind {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) (P : Cofiber f -> Type)
  (g : forall y, P (cofib f y))
  (null : exists b, forall x, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b)
  : forall x, P x.

goals.conclusion: forall x : Cofiber f, P x

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall x : Cofiber f, P x

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall b : Y, P (pushl b)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall c : Unit, P (pushr c)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall a : X, transport P (pglue a) (?pushb (f a)) = ?pushc (const_tt X a)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall b : Y, P (pushl b)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion: forall c : Unit, P (pushr c)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

goals.conclusion:
forall a : X,
transport P (pglue a) ((fun y : Y => g y) (f a)) =
(fun c : Unit => match c as u return (P (pushr u)) with
                 | tt => null.1
                 end)
  (const_tt X a)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, P (cofib f y)
{b : P (cf_apex f) & forall x : X, transport P (cfglue f x) (g (f x)) = b}

contents:
(** The homotopy cofiber can be thought of as a functor from the category of arrows in [Type] to [Type]. *)

(** 1-functorial action. *)

Definition functor_cofiber {X Y X' Y' : Type} {f : X -> Y} {f' : X' -> Y'}
  (g : X -> X') (h : Y -> Y') (p : h o f == f' o g)
  : Cofiber f -> Cofiber f'
  := functor_pushout g h idmap p (fun _ => idpath).

goals.conclusion: functor_cofiber g h p == functor_cofiber g' h' p'

hypotheses.type: h o f == f' o g
h' o f == f' o g'
forall x : X, p x @ ap f' (u x) = v (f x) @ p' x

goals.conclusion: functor_cofiber g h p == functor_cofiber g' h' p'

hypotheses.type: h o f == f' o g
h' o f == f' o g'
forall x : X, p x @ ap f' (u x) = v (f x) @ p' x

goals.conclusion:
forall a : X,
(fun x : X => 1) a @ ap (const_tt X') (u a) =
(fun x : Unit => 1) (const_tt X a) @ (fun x : X => 1) a

hypotheses.type: h o f == f' o g
h' o f == f' o g'
forall x : X, p x @ ap f' (u x) = v (f x) @ p' x

contents: (** The cofiber functor preserves the identity map. *)

Definition functor_cofiber_idmap {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y)
  : functor_cofiber (f:=f) idmap idmap (fun _ => 1) == idmap
  := functor_pushout_idmap.
(** The cofiber functor preserves composition of squares. (When mapping parallel edges). *)

(** ** Connectivity of cofibers *)

(** The cofiber of an [n]-connected map is [n.+1]-connected. *)

goals.conclusion: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f)
IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f)

forall C : Type,
In (Tr n.+1) C -> forall f0 : Cofiber f -> C, NullHomotopy f0
NullHomotopy g
forall x : Cofiber f, g x = g (cf_apex f)
forall y : Y, g (cofib f y) = g (cf_apex f)

{b : g (cf_apex f) = g (cf_apex f) &
forall x : X,
transport (fun x0 : Cofiber f => g x0 = g (cf_apex f)) 
  (cfglue f x) (?Goal (f x)) =
b}
forall y : Y, g (cofib f y) = g (cf_apex f)
forall a : X, g (cofib f (f a)) = g (cf_apex f)
g (cofib f (f x)) = g (cf_apex f)

{b : g (cf_apex f) = g (cf_apex f) &
forall x : X,
transport (fun x0 : Cofiber f => g x0 = g (cf_apex f)) 
  (cfglue f x)
  (conn_map_elim (Tr n) f (fun b0 : Y => g (cofib f b0) = g (cf_apex f))
     (fun x0 : X => ap g (cfglue f x0)) (f x)) =
b}

forall x : X,
transport (fun x0 : Cofiber f => g x0 = g (cf_apex f)) 
  (cfglue f x)
  (conn_map_elim (Tr n) f (fun b : Y => g (cofib f b) = g (cf_apex f))
     (fun x0 : X => ap g (cfglue f x0)) (f x)) =
1

transport (fun x0 : Cofiber f => g x0 = g (cf_apex f)) 
  (cfglue f x)
  (conn_map_elim (Tr n) f (fun b : Y => g (cofib f b) = g (cf_apex f))
     (fun x0 : X => ap g (cfglue f x0)) (f x)) =
1

conn_map_elim (Tr n) f (fun b : Y => g (cofib f b) = g (cf_apex f))
  (fun x0 : X => ap g (cfglue f x0)) (f x) =
ap g (cfglue f x) @ 1

conn_map_elim (Tr n) f (fun b : Y => g (cofib f b) = g (cf_apex f))
  (fun x0 : X => ap g (cfglue f x0)) (f x) =
ap g (cfglue f x)

contents:
Definition fiber_to_path_cofiber {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) (y : Y)
  : hfiber f y -> cofib f y = cf_apex f.

goals.conclusion: hfiber f y -> cofib f y = cf_apex f
hfiber f y -> cofib f y = cf_apex f
cofib f y = cf_apex f
cofib f (f x) = cf_apex f

contents: (** Blakers-Massey implies that the comparison map is highly connected.  Here we assume that [X] is merely inhabited.  There is a variant that instead assumes that [f] is surjective, with the same proof except that [blakers_massey_po] is used. *)

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1) (fiber_to_path_cofiber f y)
IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1) (fiber_to_path_cofiber f y)

contents: (* It's enough to check the connectivity of [functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)]. *)

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1) (functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f))

contents: (* We precompose with the equivalence [X <~> { y : Y & hfiber f y }]. *)

goals.conclusion:
IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1)
  (fun x : X =>
   functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
     (equiv_fibration_replacement f x))

contents: (* The Sigma-type [{ y : Y & cofib f y = cf_apex f}] in the codomain is the fiber of the map [cofib f], and so it is equivalent to the pullback of the cospan in the pushout square defining [Cofiber f].  We postcompose with this equivalence. *)

goals.conclusion:
IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1)
  ((equiv_pullback_unit_hfiber (cofib f) pushr)^-1%equiv
   o (fun x : X =>
      functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
        (equiv_fibration_replacement f x)))

contents: (* The composite is homotopic to the map from [blakers_massey_po], with the only difference being an extra [1 @ _]. *)

goals.conclusion: X -> Pullback (cofib f) pushr

?f ==
(fun x : X =>
 (equiv_pullback_unit_hfiber (cofib f) pushr)^-1%equiv
   ((fun x0 : X =>
     functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
       (equiv_fibration_replacement f x0))
      x))
IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ n).-1) ?f

pullback_corec pglue ==
(fun x : X =>
 (equiv_pullback_unit_hfiber (cofib f) pushr)^-1%equiv
   ((fun x0 : X =>
     functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
       (equiv_fibration_replacement f x0))
      x))

contents: (* Use [compute.] to see the details of the goal. *)

goals.conclusion:
pullback_corec pglue x =
(equiv_pullback_unit_hfiber (cofib f) pushr)^-1%equiv
  (functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
     (equiv_fibration_replacement f x))

pglue x =
transport (fun x0 : Y => cofib f x0 = pushr tt)
  (eisretr 1%equiv
     (functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
        (equiv_fibration_replacement f x)).1)^
  (functor_sigma idmap (fiber_to_path_cofiber f)
     (equiv_fibration_replacement f x)).2
pglue x = 1 @ cfglue f x

contents: (** This lets us prove a converse to [isconnected_cofiber] when [X] is 1-connected. *)

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr n) f
IsConnMap (Tr n) f
IsConnected (Tr (-1)) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr (-2)) f
IsConnected (Tr n.+2) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n.+1) f

hypotheses.type: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n) f

goals.conclusion: IsConnected (Tr (-1)) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr (-2)) f
IsConnected (Tr n.+2) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n.+1) f

hypotheses.type: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n) f

goals.conclusion: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (hfiber f y)

hypotheses.type: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n) f

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr n.+1) (fiber_to_path_cofiber f y)

hypotheses.type: IsConnected (Tr n.+1) (Cofiber f) -> IsConnMap (Tr n) f
